title: Replicate AI Tool Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using the Replicate AI tool, covering API and CLI usage.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Runs the GPT-4o model with streaming enabled.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason logically and verify its conclusions.
commands.scenario: When developing a complex problem-solving AI.

commands.description: Limits the response length to 150 tokens.
commands.scenario: To control API costs and response size.

commands.description: Sets the maximum context length for the model.
commands.scenario: When handling large input data for analysis.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authentication.
commands.scenario: When initializing the CLI for authenticated requests.

commands.command: Use vivid imagery to describe the scene.
commands.description: Enhances creative output by prompting for detailed descriptions.
commands.scenario: When generating creative writing or storytelling content.

commands.description: Specifies JSON as the output format for responses.
commands.scenario: When integrating with systems requiring structured data.

commands.description: Selects the latest version of the model for use.
commands.scenario: To ensure the use of the most up-to-date model features.

commands.description: Processes 10 requests in a single batch for efficiency.
commands.scenario: When optimizing for throughput in high-load environments.

commands.command: Provide a summary of the key points.
commands.description: Prompts the model to generate a concise summary.
commands.scenario: When creating executive summaries from lengthy reports.

commands.description: Sets a 30-second timeout for API requests.
commands.scenario: To prevent hanging requests in time-sensitive applications.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for troubleshooting.
commands.scenario: When diagnosing issues with API requests or responses.

commands.description: Instructs the model to format output as bullet points.
commands.scenario: When generating lists or structured content.

commands.description: Specifies the AWS region for hosting the model.
commands.scenario: To optimize latency based on geographical location.

commands.description: Implement asynchronous processing using webhooks to avoid long-polling and connection timeouts during heavy inference tasks.
Poll the prediction status (starting, processing, succeeded, failed) to implement robust error handling and state management in production pipelines.
Fine-tune the balance between generation speed and image/text quality by adjusting the iterative refinement steps in diffusion models.

commands.command: Use 'Negative Prompts' to explicitly exclude unwanted elements (e.g., 'blurry, low resolution, distorted').
commands.description: Enhance output precision by defining what the model should NOT include in the generation process.
Directly stream local binary file objects to the API to optimize multi-modal inputs without manual base64 encoding.
Asynchronous execution pattern: Use poll=False to immediately receive a prediction object without waiting for completion, preventing client-side timeouts during long-running tasks.
Advanced API parameter: Disable the default safety filter for specific research or uncensored creative workflows where content moderation might interfere with intended outputs.
Granular Webhook Control: Specify exactly which lifecycle events trigger your endpoint to reduce unnecessary server noise and optimize webhook processing logic.

commands.command: Prompt Pattern: 'Act as a [Role]. Output only the raw data in [Format] without preamble or markdown blocks.'
commands.description: Prompt Engineering: Reduces token waste and parsing errors by forcing the model to skip conversational filler (e.g., 'Here is your JSON...') which often breaks automated pipelines.
Debugging/Performance: A synchronous blocking call that simplifies local script execution by pausing the thread until the prediction is ready, useful for CLI-based testing.
